config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikorbankan hanya karena alasan kepentingan masyarakat, karena di Indonesia sendiri kita tahu betapa mudahnya “kepentingan umum” dipakai untuk membenarkan penyalahgunaan kuasa. Masyarakat memang penting, tetapi justru masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dari perlindungan hak dasar setiap orang, seperti kebebasan berpendapat, rasa aman, dan perlakuan yang adil. Prioritasnya seharusnya seimbang: kepentingan bersama penting, tetapi tidak boleh meniadakan martabat dan hak individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the idea that society should generally come before the individual, because in the United States that kind of logic has too often been used to excuse overreach by government or the majority. Public safety and the common good matter, but they are only legitimate when they are limited by constitutional rights, due process, and equal protection. If you put society first without strong restraints, you can easily trample speech, privacy, religion, and minority rights in the name of “the greater good.” To me, a healthy society is one that protects individual rights first and then builds collective solutions around them, not the other way around.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman melihat bagaimana di Indonesia alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai untuk menekan suara kritis atau merugikan kelompok kecil, saya justru curiga kalau prinsip itu dijadikan dasar utama. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, karena tanpa perlindungan individu, yang disebut “masyarakat” mudah berubah jadi alat legitimasi bagi mayoritas atau penguasa. Bagi saya, yang masuk akal adalah keseimbangan yang tegas: masyarakat kuat justru karena hak individu dihormati, bukan dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule, because in the United States that idea has repeatedly been used to justify government overreach and majority pressure. Public safety and shared interests matter, but they are only legitimate when they stay inside firm limits like constitutional rights, due process, and equal protection. I also think the point about "the common good" is often abused: once you let that phrase override basic liberties, minority rights and unpopular speech are usually the first things sacrificed. A strong society is not one that asks individuals to surrender their rights first; it is one that protects those rights and then works out collective goals within those boundaries.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus mengalahkan hak individu secara umum. Di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membungkam kritik atau mengorbankan kelompok kecil, jadi saya tidak percaya itu bisa dijadikan prinsip utama. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi oleh hak dasar, karena tanpa perlindungan individu, yang disebut masyarakat mudah berubah jadi alat kekuasaan mayoritas atau penguasa. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru lahir dari penghormatan yang tegas terhadap martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general principle. From an American perspective, that framing is dangerous because “the common good” has too often been used to justify censorship, surveillance, discrimination, and government overreach, especially against unpopular minorities. Public safety and social stability matter, but they are only legitimate when they operate inside hard limits like constitutional rights, due process, and equal protection. A healthy society is not built by asking people to surrender their rights first; it is built by protecting those rights and then pursuing collective goals within those boundaries.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
